english: Chicken Tikka with Dal Tadka and Saffron Pulao

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite Indian set meal. FOODON_00002473 (chicken dish) is used as the closest defensible anchor for the primary protein component. The full set (dal, pulao, naan) is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat, cooked — used as primary anchor for the grilled/tandoor chicken tikka component of this composite set meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a composite Indian set meal code. A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) is the closest defensible anchor for the dominant protein component. Dal, pulao, and naan sub-components are not individually coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps to a composite Indian restaurant set meal with multiple sub-components.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal serving is approximately 700 g, comprising chicken tikka (approximately 200 g), dal tadka (approximately 150 g), saffron pulao (approximately 200 g), and butter naan (approximately 150 g).

nutrient_source.title: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) — composite estimate for Indian set meal components; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredient cross-checks
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research — National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a composite Chicken Kabab Set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: chicken tikka (grilled chicken, ICMR-NIN), dal tadka (cooked toor dal, ICMR-NIN), saffron pulao (cooked basmati rice with ghee, ICMR-NIN/USDA), and butter naan (ICMR-NIN/USDA). The input record's per-100g values (energy 190 kcal, protein 17.5 g, fat 12.5 g, carbohydrate 4.0 g) are consistent with a protein-dominant kabab-weighted composite and are retained with medium confidence. Carbohydrate of 4.0 g/100g reflects the composite average across all four components at the stated 700 g portion; the kabab component dominates the per-100g profile. Values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for chicken (broilers/fryers, roasted), cooked lentils, cooked white rice, and plain naan.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database entries for basmati rice and lentils
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a composite Chicken Kabab Set. GI of 55 is estimated as a weighted average of the carbohydrate-contributing components: basmati rice (GI ~58, University of Sydney GI Database), toor dal/lentils (GI ~29, University of Sydney GI Database), and butter naan (GI ~71, analogous to white wheat bread, University of Sydney GI Database). Chicken tikka contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially affect GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (55 × 28 g carbohydrate in 700 g serving) / 100 = 15.4. Available carbohydrate per serving = 4.0 g/100g × 700 g = 28 g. A GI of 55 is classified as 'low' according to the University of Sydney GI Database (low < 55, medium 56-69, high > 70).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content of 375 mg/100g; a full 700 g set meal delivers approximately 2,625 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt in the kabab marinade and dal preparation, limit butter and ghee additions to naan and rice, avoid high-sodium chutneys or pickles as accompaniments, and consider consuming only half a set meal to keep sodium intake within safe limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite GI of approximately 55 (low) driven by basmati rice and naan; total glycemic load per full set meal is approximately 15.4, which is moderate; lentils (toor dal) contribute low-GI carbohydrate and dietary fibre that partially attenuate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should prioritise the chicken tikka and dal tadka components, limit basmati rice and naan portions to reduce glycemic load, and note that the protein-dominant per-100g profile (17.5 g protein, 4.0 g carbohydrate) is favourable when portion distribution is managed appropriately.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 3.5 g/100g (24.5 g per full 700 g set) and sodium at 375 mg/100g (2,625 mg per set) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors; cholesterol is 55 mg/100g (385 mg per set); grilling/tandoor preparation avoids deep-frying and limits additional fat uptake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to approximately 300–350 g to keep saturated fat below 12 g and sodium below 1,400 mg per meal, request reduced butter and ghee in naan and rice preparation, and favour the chicken tikka and dal components over the naan and rice components.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, ginger, garlic, cumin, garam masala) present in material quantities; grilling/tandoor preparation avoids deep-frying AGEs; saturated fat at 3.5 g/100g is below the 5 g/100g threshold of concern
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may generally consume this dish, but should request reduced butter and ghee to minimise saturated fat, and monitor response to nightshade vegetables (tomato, green chilli) if nightshade sensitivity is a concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Kabab Set is a complete Indian restaurant set meal served in Singapore, comprising four components: Chicken Tikka (marinated grilled chicken), Dal Tadka (tempered lentil curry), Saffron Pulao (fragrant saffron-infused basmati rice), and Butter Naan (tandoor-baked leavened flatbread). The chicken tikka is marinated in yogurt, turmeric, cumin, garam masala, and chilli, then grilled or cooked in a tandoor oven, yielding a smoky, charred exterior with a juicy, spiced interior. Dal Tadka is made from toor dal (split pigeon peas) simmered until soft and finished with a tempering of ghee, cumin seeds, garlic, dried red chilli, and tomato, producing a rich, earthy, mildly spiced lentil dish. Saffron Pulao is basmati rice cooked with saffron strands, whole spices, and ghee, resulting in fragrant, lightly golden rice with a delicate floral aroma. Butter Naan is a soft, slightly charred leavened flatbread baked in a tandoor and finished with butter. The per-100g nutritional profile is protein-dominant (17.5 g protein, 12.5 g fat, 4.0 g carbohydrate, 190 kcal), reflecting the kabab component's dominance in the composite average. Sodium is elevated at 375 mg/100g, making the full 700 g set meal high in sodium (approximately 2,625 mg). The composite glycemic index is approximately 55 (low), moderated by the low-GI lentil component. This dish is halal-certified and is a popular choice for Indian cuisine dining in Singapore's hawker and restaurant settings. It is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension (high sodium), type 2 diabetes (low GI, manageable with portion control), and cardiovascular disease (moderate saturated fat and high sodium at full-set portions).

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a composite Chicken Kabab Set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using ICMR-NIN (2017) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as approved sources. The input record's per-100g values are consistent with a protein-dominant kabab-weighted composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for basmati rice, toor dal, and naan. Confidence is medium due to the composite nature of the dish and the absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Indian set meal is not available in ICMR-NIN (2017) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy at the composite level as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite set meal are not available at the composite level in ICMR-NIN (2017) or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. The input record's provenance is ai_generated with confidence 0.7 from grabfood; values have been cross-checked against ICMR-NIN and USDA approved sources and are consistent with a protein-dominant composite. The two portions listed in the input (1 set meal = 700 g; 3-4 pieces seekh kabab = 200 g) are retained; the default serving for nutritional calculations is 700 g. The ethnic_tags in the input include 'Biryani' and 'Butter Chicken' which are not components of this dish; these have been removed from the output as they are inaccurate. The dish is served in Singapore as an Indian restaurant set meal and is classified as halal. Carbohydrate of 4.0 g/100g reflects the composite average across all four components weighted by the 700 g portion; the kabab component dominates the per-100g profile and suppresses the apparent carbohydrate density.
